tasks.prompt: Find CS 310 and report its title, instructor, meeting time, and seat count.
Find the instructor for CS 420.
Find the course located in Mercer Hall 340.
List all AI courses currently shown.
Identify the evening course and report its code and title.
Find the course that is full.
Find the course with the fewest remaining seats and report the seat count.
Filter the course list to show only CS courses.
Filter the course list to show only AI courses.
Filter the course list to show only evening courses.
Search for graphics and identify the matching course.
Search for systems and report all matching courses.
Apply department and time filters together to find AI courses offered in the afternoon.
Clear all filters and return to the full course list.
Add CS 101 and verify that it appears in the selected courses area.
Add CS 405 and verify that it was added successfully.
Drop CS 355 and verify that it no longer appears in the selected courses area.
Add two courses, then remove one of them and report the final selected set.
Try to add the full course and report what happens.
Open a course's detail area first, then add that course from the available controls.
Add two courses with a time conflict and report the warning that appears.
Identify a selected course that triggers a low-seat warning.
Determine whether the currently selected schedule contains a time conflict.
Add a course that creates a conflict, then remove it and confirm whether the warning disappears.
Explain why CS 310 may trigger a warning.
Determine whether the interface allows adding conflicting courses anyway.
List all currently selected courses that meet on Monday.
Identify the latest selected class in the schedule.
Identify the earliest selected class in the schedule.
Find any Friday-only class and report its code and time.
Report all selected courses in chronological order.
Determine whether any selected courses overlap on the same day.
Filter to AI courses, add one course, then confirm that it appears in the schedule.
Search for systems, add a matching course, then report its instructor and meeting time.
Add CS 452, inspect the warnings, and explain why they appear.
Build a schedule totaling exactly 8 credits.
Build a schedule with no time conflicts.
Build a schedule containing one CS course, one AI course, and one DATA course.
Find and add a highest-credit course that is not full.
Compare where alerts appear in the modern, retro, and 90s interfaces.
Compare how schedule information is accessed in the modern, retro, and 90s interfaces.
Determine which interface makes add/drop hardest and explain why.
Find the same course in all three interfaces and verify that the displayed course information matches.
Infer which selected course is causing a low-seat warning without being told directly.
In the 90s interface, load the correct course row and switch to the correct detail tab to find its alerts.
After applying an incorrect filter, recover and complete the intended course-selection task.
Handle a workflow where add/drop is only available after loading or opening a course.
Add a course and verify success using two different UI signals.
